title: eremophila subfloccosa
intro: Eremophila subfloccosa, also known as the 'Fleecy Emu Bush', is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its leaves and stems are rich in iridoids, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable resource for medicinal purposes. The health benefits associated with Eremophila subfloccosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation. Eremophila subfloccosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
